reasoning: The Pentagon initially threatened to recall Senator Mark Kelly to active duty for a court-martial but subsequently pivoted to administrative punishments (censure, attempted demotion, and pension reduction). Further, in early February 2026, a federal grand jury declined to indict him criminally. On February 12, 2026, a federal judge (Judge Richard Leon) issued a preliminary injunction outright blocking the Defense Department from punishing Kelly. While the Justice Department appealed this injunction on February 24/25, 2026, the appeals process will easily extend past the April 1, 2026 deadline. Given the active injunction, the shift away from a court-martial strategy to an administrative one, the failure to secure criminal charges, and the extremely short timeline (31 days), there is virtually no chance Kelly is recalled to Active Duty status by April 1.
